description: Description: resource selector includes `contact`.
Description: contact operation selector exposes only `create`, `get`, `list`, `update`, and `delete`.
Description: node subtitle resolves correctly when the selected resource is `contact`.
Description: contact create keeps `full_name` as a required top-level field and does not bury it inside an additional-fields collection.
Description: contact create additional-fields collection exposes `email`, `client_id`, `role`, `notes`, `is_inactive`, and `phone_numbers`.
Description: contact update additional-fields collection exposes `full_name`, `email`, `client_id`, `role`, `notes`, `is_inactive`, and `phone_numbers`.
Description: `contactId` field is shown only for contact get, update, and delete operations.
Description: contact list inputs expose `page`, `limit`, `client_id`, `search_term`, and `is_inactive`.
Load options: contact create `client_id` supports both list lookup and manual UUID entry.
Load options: contact update `client_id` supports both list lookup and manual UUID entry.
Helper: contact create payload builder maps `full_name` and omits absent optional fields.
Helper: contact create payload builder includes scalar optional fields when present.
Helper: contact update payload builder includes only provided update fields.
Helper: contact list query builder serializes pagination and core filters correctly.
Helper: `phone_numbers` parser accepts a valid JSON array of contact phone-number objects.
Helper: `phone_numbers` parser rejects malformed JSON before any request is sent.
Helper: `phone_numbers` parser rejects non-array JSON values before any request is sent.
Helper: `phone_numbers` parser rejects array entries that are missing `phone_number`.
Execute: contact create sends `POST /api/v1/contacts`.
Execute: contact create request body includes parsed `phone_numbers` when provided.
Execute: contact create unwraps a successful `{ data: ... }` response into the created contact object.
Execute: contact get sends `GET /api/v1/contacts/{id}`.
Execute: contact get returns the normalized contact object.
Execute: contact list sends `GET /api/v1/contacts` with the selected pagination and filter query parameters.
Execute: contact list preserves pagination metadata in node output.
Execute: contact update sends `PUT /api/v1/contacts/{id}` with only changed fields.
Execute: contact update returns the normalized updated contact object.
Execute: contact delete sends `DELETE /api/v1/contacts/{id}`.
Execute: contact delete returns a non-empty normalized success object containing success and deleted metadata.
Validation: contact get rejects an empty `contactId` before making a request.
Validation: contact update rejects an invalid UUID `contactId` before making a request.
Validation: contact delete rejects an invalid UUID `contactId` before making a request.
Continue-on-fail: a contact API error is returned as an item-level error object while later items still execute.
Docs: README operation matrix includes contact CRUD operations.
Docs: README describes contact field scope, lookup behavior, and list/delete output expectations.
Docs: example workflow file for contact usage is present and referenced by the README.
Docs: release notes mention the contact CRUD expansion and its first-pass scope.
